sutta: That mind monks is luminous but it is cleansed of things that come from without.
commentary: These are the two suttas that I am going to explain first these suttas are very interesting when someone is new to the Dhamma they may not notice the importance the Buddha said this mind is luminous this mind is Citta there are three words Citta vinyana and mano vinyana is consciousness mano is the thinking faculty Citta is a broader aspect of mind some say they are the same some say different aspects here Citta is luminous but defiled by things from outside these are superimposed defilements this sutta is interesting because of two views of nirvana some say all consciousness stops completely others say something remains there is a sutta where the Buddha says there is still a light the mind is luminous in the kevatta sutta the Buddha speaks of non manifestative consciousness boundless luminous consciousness this is different from the six sense consciousness this is unsupported consciousness it does not depend on objects this view is controversial hindu concept of atman is sometimes compared but their concept is not the same as buddhist atta they also reject ego and say it must be extinguished Buddha also uses the term brahma body in aggañña sutta the Buddha says tathagata is Dhammakaya brahma kaya so the ultimate state is described as brahma body this is similar in wording to hindu brahma the meaning of this sutta is that the mind is originally bright but defilements from outside darken it ordinary people see darkness when eyes are closed arahants experience brightness thai forest teachers speak of original mind as bright defilements arise through six sense consciousness energy is scattered through senses this causes the mind to become dark through samādhi and jhāna the mind becomes one pointed energy is no longer scattered the mind regains luminosity jhāna is mental brightness this sutta supports the view of original luminosity but we must be careful about claims of existence or non existence in nirvana the Buddha said do not say it exists or does not exist it is beyond the mind it can be experienced but not known by ordinary mind.
